field.markdownDescription:
Lists level severity terms

Specify the field name to assign with the inferred severity level (Default: level)

terms.markdownDescription:
Lists level severity terms

Specify severity level classification, start-of-line matching. Used to identify the severity level (TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, CRITICAL) of a log line,  typically marking the start of a log event (head). Sorted by likelihood and uniqueness. Used in a Trie for O(m) matching.  For example:  ``` yaml  - 'panic=CRITICAL'       # Go panic, first line; e.g., "panic: runtime error: index out of range"  - 'fatal_error=CRITICAL' # Go fatal error, first line; e.g., "fatal error: all goroutines are asleep"  - 'thread_''main''_panicked_at=CRITICAL' # Rust panic, first line; e.g., "thread 'main' panicked at 'explicit panic'"  - 'Segmentation_fault=CRITICAL' # C/C++ memory violation, first line; e.g., "Segmentation fault (core dumped)" ```

timestampPatterns.markdownDescription:
Lists timestamp patterns associated with severity level

Lists the terms assigned to a target TenXObject's [levelField](https://doc.log10x.com/run/initialize/level/#levelField) based on whether its [fullText](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+fullText) contains specified terms. For example: ``` yaml - "I'MMdd HH:mm:ss.S=INFO"          # Used in Kubernetes kube-apiserver logs with INFO prefix, 1-digit microsecond precision ```
